channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PDP Grants Goodluck Jonathan Automatic Clearance as Presidential Candidate for 2027
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) faction wey Kabiru Turaki, SAN dey lead don give former President Goodluck Jonathan waiver from di usual presidential screening ahead of di 2027 election. As e be no...

item.title: The Prodigy to return to New York for first live show since 2009
item.description: The Prodigy, the iconic electronic dance band, have confirmed their first New York performance in 17 years, scheduled for Friday, 25th September 2026, at Under The K Bridge in Brooklyn. This concert w...

item.title: Analysis of Prime Minister Carney's Approach to Middle East Relations
item.description: On May 7, Prime Minister Mark Carney spoke with Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas, expressing criticism of Israel while praising unspecified reforms by the Palestinian Authority (PA). Crit...

item.title: Government and matatu owners reach temporary agreement to suspend strike after fuel price talks
item.description: On Monday and Tuesday, government officials including Interior CS Kipchumba Murkomen, Energy CS Opiyo Wandayi, National Treasury CS John Mbadi and Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja held closed-door meet...

item.title: Phala Phala Scandal Highlights the Importance of Accountability and Citizen Vigilance in South Africa
item.description: The Phala Phala scandal involving President Cyril Ramaphosa has reignited debates on governance, accountability, and the ethical expectations of South African citizens. While much attention is on the ...

item.title: Vancouver Canucks make major coaching changes following disappointing season
item.description: The Vancouver Canucks announced significant changes to their coaching staff on May 19, 2026. Head coach Adam Foote, along with assistant coaches Scott Young, Kevin Dean, and Brett McLean, were relieve...

item.title: Court orders destruction of vessel used to smuggle Sh8.2 billion worth of meth
item.description: Kenyan authorities have moved to destroy a vessel that was found carrying a large consignment of methamphetamine valued at about Sh8.2 billion, following its seizure in a multi-agency operation off th...

item.title: Finance Bank of Malawi seeks compensation following Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Finance Bank of Malawi, which ceased operations in 2005, is pursuing compensation from the Malawian government amounting to over K1.02 trillion (approximately R9 billion) after the Supreme Court of Ap...

item.title: Robin Padilla Confirms He Was with Senator Dela Rosa During Senate Incident
item.description: Senator Robinhood Padilla has stated that he has not yet received a summons to explain his presence with Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa during the early morning hours of May 14 when Dela Rosa left th...

item.title: Tanzania Meteorological Authority Issues Five-Day Strong Wind Alert for Coastal Regions
item.description: The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has issued a warning about strong winds that are expected to affect various coastal regions starting Wednesday, May 20, 2026, and continuing for five days. ...

item.title: Trump-endorsed Senate changes could stall GOP legislative agenda, analysts warn
item.description: President Donald Trump's recent efforts to remove Republican senators he considers insufficiently loyal may have unintended consequences for the GOP's control of the Senate. Trump-backed challengers s...

item.title: ASUS Positions Kenya as East Africa Gateway for Affordable AI Devices Targeting Youth and SMEs
item.description: ASUS is making serious moves in Kenya by tapping into the country's energetic tech-savvy youths and the growing number of SMEs that are digitising their operations. The Taiwanese company sees Kenya as...

item.title: Seventh NBC Dodoma Marathon Aims to Attract Record 15,000 Participants
item.description: The seventh edition of the NBC Dodoma Marathon has officially started, bringing together running enthusiasts from across Tanzania. This year, the organisers are aiming for a record participation of 15...

item.title: Supreme Court to review NSA detention of journalist Satyam Verma linked to Noida workers protest
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has agreed to examine a petition challenging the preventive detention of former journalist Satyam Verma under the National Security Act in connection with the Noida workers’...

item.title: Fatal three-car collision in Kemps Creek claims lives of man and infant
item.description: A tragic traffic accident occurred in Kemps Creek, New South Wales, resulting in the deaths of a man and an infant. The incident involved three vehicles and has shocked the local community. Emergency ...

item.title: Young Entrepreneurs Raise Concerns Over Proposed Capital Gains Tax Changes
item.description: A group of 40 Australian entrepreneurs under 40 has voiced concerns about the Albanese government's proposed capital gains tax reforms. While the young business leaders support measures targeting nega...

item.title: Merle Lynn: From Iconic 1960s Model to Published Novelist
item.description: Merle Lynn, born in February 1943, was a leading figure in the high-fashion world of the 1960s. Her elegance and poise made her a favorite for renowned photographers such as Richard Dormer and Guy Bou...

item.title: Childhood Stunting in South Africa: The Critical Role of Gut Health Beyond Nutrition
item.description: South Africa faces a paradox where supermarkets are stocked yet childhood stunting remains prevalent. Despite global increases in food production and wealth, approximately 150-million children under f...

item.title: PM Nchemba calls for reform on bank loan collateral sales
item.description: Dodoma. Tanzania Prime Minister Dr Mwigulu Nchemba has openly criticised local banks for selling borrowers’ collateral without taking into account the remaining loan balance, calling the practice unju...

item.title: SAHRC investigates corruption and mismanagement in Gauteng water services
item.description: The South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) has begun a three-day public inquiry into Gauteng’s worsening water crisis, citing corruption, poor management, and failing infrastructure as key issu...
